clause_quote: This Extended Mechanical Protection policy applies only to vehicles with an active extended coverage mapping recorded against the VIN.
Extended coverage applies to the listed steering, suspension, climate control, and infotainment components after the base warranty period has ended.
If no active extended protection mapping exists for the VIN, the claim is not eligible under this policy.
Extended Mechanical Protection provides coverage through 72 months from the in-service date or 140,000 km, whichever occurs first.
A claim is within the time limit when the repair order date is on or before the recorded contract end date.
A claim is within the mileage limit when the odometer reading is at or below the recorded contract mileage ceiling.
Covered extended protection components include steering racks, front struts, rear shock absorbers, air conditioning compressors, and infotainment head units when listed in the product definition.
The repair code and stated causal part must correspond to a covered extended protection component.
A repair code and causal part mismatch is not eligible for automatic approval.
Extended protection applies only to defects in material or workmanship and does not cover ordinary deterioration.
The claim description must identify the complaint, the defect found, and the repair performed.
If the claim description does not clearly establish a defect condition, the claim requires more information.
Extended Mechanical Protection does not cover engine assembly failures, corrosion, cosmetic concerns, tyres, or normal wear items.
Failures caused by abuse, collision damage, unauthorized modification, or lack of maintenance are excluded from extended protection.
If an excluded cause is indicated but not conclusively established, the claim shall be referred for human review.
Every extended protection claim must include the VIN, in-service date, repair order date, odometer reading, repair code, causal part, and a specific failure description.
Electronic and infotainment claims require a diagnostic report supporting the claimed fault condition.
Claims with missing mandatory documentation shall not be approved and require completion before payment.
Labor reimbursement is evaluated against the published standard and maximum labor time for the repair code.
A claim within the published labor limit may proceed when all other coverage conditions are satisfied.
Labor time above the published maximum requires human review and cannot be auto-approved.
Extended protection is valid only while the contract status remains active and not cancelled or expired.
If the contract expired before the repair order date, the claim is outside coverage.
If contract status data is unclear or conflicting, the claim shall be referred for human review.
An extended protection claim shall be approved only when coverage, failure eligibility, documentation, contract status, and labor limits are all satisfied.
An extended protection claim shall be rejected when the contract period has ended, the mileage limit has been exceeded, or an exclusion applies.
An extended protection claim shall be referred when the evidence is incomplete, conflicting, or unusually ambiguous.
Goodwill assistance under an expired mechanical contract may be considered only for a small time or mileage overrun when no exclusion applies.
Goodwill requires a service history consistent with proper maintenance and a defect pattern that resembles a warrantable failure.
Goodwill cannot be auto-approved and must be referred for human authorization.
